description: A wind instrument producing sound from air blown across an opening; music made from breath. 'Phui' names what sings breath—air becoming melody.
sound_symbolism: The word breathes high. 'Phu' /ɸu/ blows with gentle breath, air moving. Then 'i' rises clear and high, note ascending.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'phui' (flute), 'lo phui' (flutes). Related to 'kuma' (drum): the drum carries rhythm, 'phui' carries melody.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Breath as art—the flute transforms exhale into beauty, body becoming instrument.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Flutes were first music—bone, reed, bamboo, the oldest instruments found in human sites.
